channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Bronny James has a left heel bruise and is listed as doubtful for the Lakers' next game
item.description: EL SEGUNDO, Calif. (AP) — Bronny James has a bruised left heel, and he is listed as doubtful for the Los Angeles Lakers' next game against the Utah Jazz on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Debris falls from roof at home of Cowboys before game against Texans; no injuries reported
item.description: Officials say a piece of the roof at the home of the Dallas Cowboys fell to the field while the retractable portion of it was being opened at least three hours before a game against Houston on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bay Area girls volleyball team advances to NorCal final via forfeit as opponent cites ‘male athlete’ concern
item.description: San Francisco Waldorf girls volleyball team advances to NorCal final via forfeit as Stone Ridge Christian cites ‘male athlete’ concern
item.dc:creator: Nathan Canilao, The Mercury News

item.title: Rory McIlroy brought to tears talking about rough year, including almost divorcing wife, after latest win
item.description: Rory McIlroy has had some big wins, but he was brought to tears after winning his latest trophy as he reflected on a year of adversity, including almost getting divorced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kansas remains No. 1 in AP poll, top 10 shuffled with losses by 3 teams; Wisconsin joins rankings
item.description: Kansas strengthened its grip on No. 1 in the AP Top 25. The rest of the top 10 turned into a jumble of teams swapping places. The Jayhawks picked up five first-place votes in The Associated Press men's college basketball poll released on Monday, receiving 49 from a 61-person media panel. No.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump thinks he can fix golf's mess. He starts by playing golf with PGA Tour commissioner
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump boasted during the campaign he would need about 15 minutes to get a deal done between the PGA Tour and the Saudi investors of LIV Golf. That started with four hours on the golf course with PGA Tour Commissioner Jay Monahan.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suzuki and Sabathia among 14 newcomers on baseball Hall of Fame ballot. Wagner tops holdovers
item.description: COOPERSTOWN, N.Y. (AP) — Outfielder Ichiro Suzuki and pitcher CC Sabathia are among 14 new candidates on the Hall of Fame ballot released Monday, joining 14 holdovers led by reliever Billy Wagner.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minute Maid Park in Houston will soon be renamed Daikin Park under new business partnership
item.description: The Houston Astros’ home will get a new name on Jan. 1, becoming Daikin Park under an agreement through the 2039 season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Ahmed Humble, KPRC 2

item.title: CFL Grey Cup ref makes rookie mistake at coin toss
item.description: Canadian Football League referee Ben Major suffered a faux pas during the coin toss of the Grey Cup on Sunday between the Toronto Argonauts and the Winnipeg Blue Bombers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jazz comeback falls short against Clippers, 116-105
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - James Harden moved into second place on the NBA's career 3-point list and had 20 points and 11 assists to help the Los Angeles Clippers beat the Utah Jazz 116-105 on Sunday night to end a three-game losing streak. Harden moved past Ray Allen with No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: James Harden breaks tie with Ray Allen, moves to No. 2 on NBA's career 3-point list
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — James Harden now stands alone at No. 2 in 3-pointers. The Los Angeles Clippers guard connected on the 2,974th 3-pointer of his career Sunday night and broke a tie with Basketball Hall of Famer Ray Allen for the No. 2 spot on the NBA’s list.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: NBA fines Bucks coach Doc Rivers for comments about call refs admitted they got wrong
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Doc Rivers was right. It still cost him $25,000. Rivers, the coach of the Milwaukee Bucks, was fined by the NBA on Sunday, a day after he publicly criticized the officiating that led to the deciding points in a 115-114 loss to the Charlotte Hornets.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jordan Love runs for go-ahead TD and Packers beat Bears on Karl Brooks' FG block at buzzer
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Jordan Love and the Green Bay Packers made just enough plays to squeeze out yet another win over the Chicago Bears. This time, it took a late touchdown and a blocked field goal.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blackhawks place defenseman Seth Jones on injured reserve with right foot injury
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — The Blackhawks placed defenseman Seth Jones on injured reserve Saturday with a right foot injury. The 30-year-old Jones had played in each of Chicago's first 17 games, going into Saturday's loss in Vancouver, averaging more than 25 1/2 minutes of ice time.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netflix experiences streaming delays leading up to Tyson-Paul fight
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Netflix's first attempt at handling a live sports event did not receive a passing grade. The fight Friday night between Mike Tyson and Jake Paul experienced streaming problems according to many viewers on social media.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US skier Mikaela Shiffrin wins 1st World Cup slalom of season for her 98th career victory
item.description: American ski star Mikaela Shiffrin earned her record-extending 98th career World Cup win Saturday to give herself the chance to compete for victory No. 100 in front of a home crowd.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Donovan Mitchell scores 37, Cavaliers stay perfect and improve to 14-0 with 144-126 win over Bulls
item.description: Donovan Mitchell scored a season-high 37 points, Darius Garland had 29 and the Cleveland Cavaliers continued one of the best starts in league history, improving to 14-0 with a 144-126 win over the Chicago Bulls on Friday night in an NBA Cup game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bountiful headed back to 5A championship game
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Bountiful Redhawks are headed back to the 5A championship game. Bountiful cruised past Brighton in the 5A semifinals Friday at Rice-Eccles Stadium, 24-8, and will take on Roy for the 5A state title next week.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Girls Volleyball: Cathedral 3, Los Alamitos 0
item.description: The Lady Dons take on the Griffins of Los Alamitos in the first round of the 2024 CIF Open Division Championships.
item.dc:creator: Joaquin Duncan, Fox 5

item.title: Olympic champion Lindsey Vonn is ending her retirement at age 40 to make a skiing comeback
item.description: Lindsey Vonn is coming out of retirement to rejoin the U.S. Ski Team, she announced Thursday, intending to race again at age 40 — and six years after her last Olympics.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: San Diego Gulls to host Military Appreciation Night this Saturday
item.description: Get ready for an exciting evening of hockey, entertainment and heartfelt gratitude this Saturday, Nov. 16, as the San Diego Gulls host their Military Appreciation Night.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Mitchell Chaffee's journey to the Tampa Bay Lightning
item.description: Making it to the NHL is a huge accomplishment for forward Mitchell Chaffee, especially as an undrafted player. Not only did he beat the odds, he’s overcome a serious injury.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utes quarterback Brandon Rose out for the season
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - In a year that has been devasted by season-ending injuries, the Utes football team has suffered another one. After making his first career start, quarterback Brandon Rose has been lost for the year after suffering a lower leg injury in Saturday's 22-21 loss to BYU.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: BYU ranked 6th in College Football Playoff poll
item.description: PROVO, Utah (AB4 Sports) - It was one of the most thrilling victories in BYU history, and will go down as a true rivalry classic. But perhaps what was even more thrilling for the Cougars than their 22-21 win over Utah Saturday night, was the reception they received late Saturday night in Provo.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bears fire offensive coordinator Shane Waldron
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — The Chicago Bears have fired offensive coordinator Shane Waldron. The club has promoted passing game coordinator Thomas Brown to offensive coordinator.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9
